question_title: Which month will have the highest officially reported TSMC consolidated net revenue among January, February, and March 2026?
summary:
This question asks which month in Q1 2026 has the highest TSMC consolidated net revenue. January (NT$401,255M) and February (NT$317,657M) are already reported. March is not yet reported but is implied by Q1 guidance ($34.6-35.8B). February is effectively eliminated at NT$317.7B. The race is between January and March.

At Q1 guidance midpoint ($35.2B) with the guided FX rate (31.6), March would be ~NT$393B - below January. However, two factors push March higher: (1) TSMC's tendency to beat guidance modestly, and (2) the TWD has weakened to ~32.0 vs the 31.6 assumption, boosting TWD revenue. Monte Carlo simulations with various parameter assumptions show probabilities ranging from 35-65% for each of January and March depending on the Q1 beat magnitude and actual FX rate. Historical Q1 seasonality favors January (highest in both 2024 and 2025). This is genuinely close to a coin flip with a very slight edge to January based on historical patterns and the midpoint guidance calculation.

factors.description: January 2026 already reported at NT$401,255M - record high, confirmed data point that March must exceed
February 2026 at NT$317,657M is ~21% below January due to Lunar New Year effect - effectively eliminated
Historical Q1 seasonality: January was highest Q1 month in both 2024 (36.4% of Q1) and 2025 (34.9% of Q1)
Q1 guidance midpoint ($35.2B at guided FX 31.6) implies March ~NT$393B, below January's NT$401B
TSMC tends to beat guidance modestly - could push Q1 revenue above midpoint, raising March revenue
TWD weakened to ~32.0 in March vs guided 31.6, boosting March TWD-denominated revenue by ~1.3%
Strong AI demand with full capacity utilization and 2nm ramp could push March above typical seasonal pattern

reasoning:
**Summary of my forecast:**

| Option | Probability |
|--------|-------------|
| January 2026 | **50%** |
| February 2026 | **1%** |
| March 2026 | **49%** |

**Key reasoning:**

- **January** (NT$401,255M) and **February** (NT$317,657M) are already reported. February is far too low to win.
- **March** is not yet reported. Using TSMC's Q1 guidance ($34.6-35.8B USD) minus Jan+Feb actuals, March is implied at NT$374-412B.
- At guidance **midpoint** with the guided FX rate (31.6), March would be ~NT$393B — below January. January wins.
- However, the TWD has **weakened to ~32.0** and TSMC has a **tendency to beat guidance**, both of which push March higher. At $35.4B Q1 with 31.8 FX, March likely exceeds January.
- **Historical seasonality** favors January (highest Q1 month in 2024 and 2025).
- This is genuinely a coin flip, with sensitivity analysis showing 35-65% for each outcome depending on assumptions. I give a very slight edge to January based on the guidance midpoint anchor and historical pattern.
sources_consulted: [TSMC 2026 monthly revenue - Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited](https://investor.tsmc.com/english/monthly-revenue/2026)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to the option whose month has the highest consolidated net revenue among January, February, and March 2026, using TSMC’s official 2026 Monthly Revenue page at https://investor.tsmc.com/english/monthly-revenue/2026  In case of tie, the tiebreaker is the month occuring sooner
fine_print: Compare the monthly consolidated net revenue values exactly as reported by TSMC. Use the figures on the 2026 Monthly Revenue page; if that page is temporarily unavailable, use the same month values from the first replacement investor disclosure that TSMC posts. Candidate Sources: TSMC Financial Calendar https://investor.tsmc.com/english/financial-calendar
